display.purpose: Page titles only. One per page.

headline.purpose: Section titles inside long-form pages (About, Uses).

title.purpose: Card titles, note titles, resume company names.

eyebrow.purpose: Metadata leading a card: dates, roles, status labels.

label.purpose: Badge text and small status flags.

mono.purpose: Photo overlay captions; code blocks via prose plugin.

shadows.name: nav-lift
shadows.purpose: Desktop nav pill and mobile nav button. Supports the backdrop-blur, not an announcement of elevation.

shadows.name: avatar-emboss
shadows.purpose: Small circular avatars in the resume timeline. A whisper of depth for round-on-rectangle.

motion.name: default-transition
motion.purpose: Tailwind default transition; hover color shifts on links and badges.

motion.name: card-halo-reveal
motion.purpose: The hover halo behind cards: scale 0.95 to 1, opacity 0 to 1.

motion.name: photo-straighten
motion.purpose: Photos strip on hover: rotate to 0, scale to 1.1.

motion.name: nav-panel-enter
motion.purpose: Mobile nav panel: fade and scale from 0.95.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Explicit action button. Zinc-on-zinc, no shadow, softly rounded.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: #27272a; color: #f4f4f5; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 12px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; outline-offset: 2px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #3f3f46; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #27272a; color: rgba(244, 244, 245, 0.7); transition: none; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FB2576; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Subordinate action. Light zinc tile, dark text.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: #fafafa; color: #18181b; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 8px 12px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; outline-offset: 2px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #f4f4f5; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FB2576; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tag Badge
components.description: Pill-shaped clickable tag. Hover shifts text to Signal Magenta; background stays zinc.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #f4f4f5; color: #52525b; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1rem; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-badge:hover { color: #FB2576; } .ds-badge:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FB2576; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Non-interactive state flag (used for "Work in progress" on draft notes). Magenta fill, rectangular not pill so it reads as a state, not a tag.
components.css: .ds-badge-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #FB2576; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 0 8px; border-radius: 6px; }

components.name: Nav Pill
components.description: Desktop navigation. Rounded-full, semi-opaque white with backdrop-blur, supported by a faint nav-lift shadow. Active link colors Signal Magenta; no underline, no background change.
components.html:
• Home
  • Notes
  • Creating
  • Uses
  • About
components.css: .ds-nav ul { display: flex; list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0 12px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); border-radius: 9999px; box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(39, 39, 42, 0.05), 0 4px 6px -4px rgba(39, 39, 42, 0.05); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(8px); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(24, 24, 27, 0.05), 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(39, 39, 42, 0.05); } .ds-nav a { display: block; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #27272a; text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-nav a:hover, .ds-nav a.is-active { color: #FB2576; } .ds-nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FB2576; outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 9999px; }

components.name: Note Card
components.description: The signature card pattern. No visible chrome at rest; a tonal halo appears on hover, behind the content, with a small scale-up. Whole card is clickable via an absolute link overlay. Eyebrow uses the 2px pip metadata leader.
components.html:
April 12, 2024

Notes on shipping faster than you think

A short collection of things I keep relearning about scope, momentum, and the gap between draft and done.

Read note →
components.css: .ds-card { position: relative; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: flex-start; padding: 24px 0; cursor: pointer; } .ds-card-halo { position: absolute; inset: 0 -24px; background: #fafafa; border-radius: 16px; opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.95); transition: opacity 200ms ease-out, transform 200ms ease-out; z-index: 0; } .ds-card:hover .ds-card-halo { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } .ds-card-inner { position: relative; z-index: 1; } .ds-card-eyebrow { display: flex; align-items: center; margin: 0 0 12px; padding-left: 14px; position: relative; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #71717a; } .ds-card-pip { position: absolute; left: 0; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%); display: block; width: 2px; height: 16px; background: #e4e4e7; border-radius: 9999px; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #27272a; text-wrap: balance; } .ds-card-title a { color: inherit; text-decoration: none; } .ds-card-title a::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0 -24px; border-radius: 16px; z-index: 2; } .ds-card-desc { margin: 0 0 16px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5rem; color: #52525b; } .ds-card-cta { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; margin: 0; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #FB2576; }

components.name: Page Title
components.description: The single Display heading allowed per page. Bold, tight tracking, text-balanced.
components.css: .ds-page-title { font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 2.5rem; line-height: 1; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #27272a; text-wrap: balance; margin: 0; }

components.name: Resume Card
components.description: The only visibly-bordered container in the system. A 16px-radius zinc-100 border holds a 4-6 row list of recent work. Each row: 40px ring-bordered logo, company name, right-aligned date range, role on its own line.
components.html:
Work

  1. M
    Motion2025 — PresentSenior Software Engineer
  2. F
    Film.io2024 — 2025Software Architect
components.css: .ds-resume { max-width: 420px; border: 1px solid #f4f4f5; border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; } .ds-resume-heading { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; margin: 0 0 24px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #27272a; } .ds-resume-icon { width: 24px; height: 24px; flex: none; } .ds-resume-list { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; } .ds-resume-list li { display: flex; gap: 16px; align-items: flex-start; } .ds-resume-logo { width: 40px; height: 40px; flex: none; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #fafafa; border-radius: 9999px; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(24, 24, 27, 0.05), 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(39, 39, 42, 0.05); font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #27272a; margin-top: 4px; } .ds-resume-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 4px 8px; flex: 1; } .ds-resume-company { font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #27272a; flex: none; } .ds-resume-dates { margin-left: auto; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #a1a1aa; flex: none; } .ds-resume-role { width: 100%; font-family: 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #71717a; }

narrative.overview: A lined notebook on a working engineer's desk. Most of the page is paper: zinc-toned, lightly contrasted, given over to text. One pen is permitted, a sharp magenta, used only to mark what matters. The chrome is restrained to the point of near-invisibility (a ring of one pixel, a tonal halo on hover); the typography carries the weight of the room. Personal but not precious, considered but never showy. The system sits in opposition to three things: the generic Next.js-plus-Tailwind dev portfolio (hero, three cards, CTA), the corporate SaaS landing page (gradient hero, feature grid, logo wall), and the Dribbble showpiece (motion-for-motion's-sake, form over substance). It is also explicitly not magazine-grade essayist territory; the voice is an engineer's notebook, not a literary one. Density is moderate. Spacing breathes on larger viewports; type does the structural work that borders and fills do in louder systems. Motion exists only as a response to reading: a hover that reveals a halo behind a card, a scroll that scales an avatar, a photo that straightens when you point at it. Nothing performs.
keyCharacteristics: Zinc-and-magenta, dual-theme, parallel rules across light and dark
Geist Sans plus Geist Mono; a single family pair carries the entire site
Flat by default; depth from ring-1 tonal contrast, not shadow
The accent is a signal, not a wash; under 10% of any view
Interaction reveals; the resting state is quiet

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Magenta appears on at most 10% of any rendered view, and only where it announces state or action: an active link, a CTA arrow, a status flag. It is never used decoratively (no gradient washes, no accent rules, no tinted backgrounds, no 'magenta because it looks nice'). The color's rarity is the point.

rules.name: The Tonal Border Rule
rules.body: Borders are ring-1 at low opacity (ring-zinc-900/5 or ring-white/10), never a full-saturation stroke. If a border reads as a line, it is too loud. If you cannot see it, it is correct.

rules.name: The Two-Themes-One-Voice Rule
rules.body: Light and dark are not variants of each other; they are parallel realizations of one system. Every named color has both forms. A component that ships only one theme is incomplete.

rules.name: The Tracking-Tight Rule
rules.body: Headings (Display, Headline, Title) carry tracking-tight. Body text is normal tracking. The contrast between tight headings and loose body is part of the rhythm; flat tracking across both reads as uncommitted.

rules.name: The Text-Balance Rule
rules.body: Page titles and card titles use text-wrap: balance (the local .text-balance utility). Headings that wrap to two lines should never strand a single short word on the second line.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Geist Sans is the only display+body family. Reaching for a second sans, a serif, or a script breaks the workshop register. Mono is permitted but scoped to code and photo captions.

rules.name: The Tonal-Before-Shadow Rule
rules.body: When reaching for depth, reach for ring-1 with a low-opacity zinc before reaching for box-shadow. The system already commits to flatness; shadow is the second answer, not the first.

rules.name: The Backdrop-Blur-Earns-Its-Lift Rule
rules.body: The nav pill is the only element permitted to float. Other floating UI (modals, popovers) inherits the same backdrop-blur language so the lift mechanic is one idea applied twice, not two competing ideas.

dos: Do keep Signal Magenta (#FB2576) under 10% of any rendered view. The Signal Rule is non-negotiable; the magenta only marks state or action.
Do reach for ring-1 with low-opacity zinc before reaching for box-shadow. Tonal contrast first, shadow second. Flat is the default.
Do ship both light and dark forms for every component. A one-theme component is incomplete.
Do use tracking-tight on headings (Display, Headline, Title) and normal tracking on body. The contrast is part of the rhythm.
Do use text-wrap: balance on page titles and card titles; do not strand a single word on the second line.
Do reveal interaction state through tonal halos and motion, not through additional chrome (no extra borders, no fills, no shadows).
Do use Geist Sans for everything; reserve Geist Mono for code and the photo-strip captions.
donts: Don't add a second display family or a serif. The One-Family Rule holds.
Don't use Signal Magenta as a background fill, a gradient wash, or a decorative accent. It is a pen, not a paint.
Don't ship a gradient hero, a feature-card grid, a logo wall, a 'Trusted by' row, or a testimonial slider. These read as corporate SaaS landing; the site is explicitly not that.
Don't ship the generic Next.js + Tailwind dev-portfolio silhouette (centered hero with avatar, three identical project cards, a single CTA). The whole point is to stop reading as that.

Don't ship motion that performs without serving the reader. The Photos hover, the card halo, and the scroll-driven avatar earn their motion by making content easier to engage with. New motion needs the same justification or it slides into Dribbble-showpiece territory.

Don't apply border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts. The 2px Eyebrow pip is the single exception and it is a metadata leader, not a card stripe.
Don't apply background-clip: text with a gradient. Headings are solid color, emphasis through weight and size.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively. The nav pill's backdrop-blur exists because the pill floats over content; other surfaces do not get to imitate it.
Don't introduce a feature-card grid of identical icon+heading+text tiles. The site never repeats a card pattern.
Don't reach for a modal as the first answer. Inline disclosure and route-level pages are the defaults.
Don't use em dashes in copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
